words.def: a curved piece of glass or plastic that makes things look larger, smaller or clearer when you look through it
words.example: a pair of glasses with tinted lenses

words.def: having a flat surface that does not slope
words.example: Pitch the tent on level ground.

words.def: an official document that shows that permission has been given to do, own or use something
words.example: Applicants must hold a valid driving licence.

words.def: the length of time that somebody lives or that something lasts
words.example: a lifetime of experience

words.def: the arrangement or type of light in a place
words.example: electric/natural lighting

words.def: the same; in a similar way
words.example: He voted for the change and he expected his colleagues to do likewise.

words.def: the act or process of limiting or controlling somebody/something
words.example: They would resist any limitation of their powers.

words.def: not very great in amount or extent
words.example: We are doing our best with the limited resources available.

words.def: a long, thin mark on a surface
words.example: a straight/solid/dotted/dashed line

words.def: a person who listens
words.example: a good listener (= somebody who you can rely on to listen with attention or sympathy)

words.def: in a literal way
words.example: The word ‘planet’ literally means ‘wandering body’.

words.def: connected with literature
words.example: literary criticism/theory

words.def: a unit for measuring volume, equal to 1.76 British pints or 2.11 American pints
words.example: 3 litres of water

words.def: small pieces of rubbish such as paper, cans and bottles, that people have left lying in a public place
words.example: There will be fines for people who drop litter.

words.def: full of life and energy; active and enthusiastic
words.example: an intelligent and lively young woman

words.def: something that is being carried (usually in large amounts) by a person, vehicle, etc.
words.example: The trucks waited at the warehouse to pick up their loads.

words.def: money that an organization such as a bank lends and somebody borrows
words.example: to take out/repay a loan (= to borrow money/pay it back)

words.def: seeming natural, reasonable or sensible
words.example: It was a logical conclusion from the child's point of view.

words.def: a printed design or symbol that a company or an organization uses as its special sign
words.example: All over the world there are red and white paper cups bearing the company logo.

words.def: lasting or having an effect over a long period of time
words.example: Our long-term goal is to lower operating costs by 10 per cent.

words.def: not securely fixed where it should be; able to become separated from something
words.example: a loose button/tooth

words.def: (in the UK) a man of high rank in the nobility (= people of high social class), or somebody who has been given the title ‘lord’ as an honour
words.example: She's married to a lord.

words.def: a way of raising money for a government, charity, etc. by selling tickets that have different numbers on them that people have chosen. Numbers are then chosen by chance and the people who have those numbers on their tickets win prizes.
words.example: the national/state lottery

words.def: not high or tall; not far above the ground
words.example: a low wall/building/table

words.def: to reduce something or to become less in value, quality, etc.
words.example: lower something, He lowered his voice to a whisper.

words.def: remaining constant in your support of somebody/something
words.example: a loyal friend/supporter

words.def: either of the two organs in the chest that you use for breathing
words.example: Her father died of lung cancer.

words.def: expensive and of very high quality
words.example: a luxury hotel/car/apartment

words.def: the words of a popular song
words.example: music and lyrics by Rodgers and Hart

words.def: extremely attractive and impressive; deserving praise
words.example: The Taj Mahal is a magnificent building.

words.def: to make something continue at the same level, standard, etc.
words.example: to maintain law and order/standards/a balance/control

words.def: the largest part of a group of people or things
words.example: majority (of somebody/something), The majority of people interviewed prefer TV to radio.

words.def: the name or type of a machine, piece of equipment, etc. that is made by a particular company
words.example: make of something, What make of car does he drive?

words.def: coloured substances used on the skin to make yourself look more attractive or to change your appearance
words.example: eye make-up

words.def: the act or process of making or producing something
words.example: strategic decision-making

words.def: to make goods in large quantities, using machines
words.example: manufactured goods

words.def: the business or industry of producing goods in large quantities in factories, etc.
words.example: Many jobs in manufacturing were lost during the recession.

words.def: a drawing or plan of the earth’s surface or part of it, showing countries, towns, rivers, etc.
words.example: a map of France

words.def: a long running race of about 42 kilometres or 26 miles
words.example: the London marathon

words.def: the empty space at the side of a written or printed page
words.example: the left-hand/right-hand margin

words.def: an object or a sign that shows the position of something
words.example: a boundary marker

words.def: the activity of presenting, advertising and selling a company’s products or services in the best possible way
words.example: a marketing campaign/strategy

words.def: affecting or involving a large number of people or things
words.example: The world faces the tremendous problem of mass unemployment.

words.def: very large, heavy and solid
words.example: a massive rock

words.def: a man who has people working for him, often as servants in his home
words.example: They lived in fear of their master.

words.def: having the same colour, pattern, style, etc. and therefore looking attractive together
words.example: The two sisters wore matching outfits.

words.def: a friend
words.example: They've been best mates since school.

words.def: connected with money, possessions, etc. rather than with the needs of the mind or spirit
words.example: material possessions/wealth

words.def: as large, fast, etc. as is possible, or the most that is possible or allowed
words.example: the maximum amount/number of something

words.def: the head of the government of a town or city, etc., elected by the public
words.example: the Mayor of New York

words.def: an action, an object or a system by which a result is achieved; a way of achieving or doing something
words.example: means of something, Email is a highly effective means of communication.

words.def: the act or the process of finding the size, quantity or degree of something
words.example: the metric system of measurement

words.def: a person whose job is repairing machines, especially the engines of vehicles
words.example: a car/motor mechanic

words.def: operated by power from an engine
words.example: a mechanical device/toy/clock

words.def: a set of moving parts in a machine that performs a task
words.example: a delicate watch mechanism

words.def: a flat piece of metal, usually like a coin in shape, that is given to the winner of a competition or to somebody who has been brave, for example in war
words.example: to win a gold medal in the Olympics

words.def: the main ways that large numbers of people receive information and entertainment, that is television, radio, newspapers and the internet
words.example: the national/international media

words.def: a drug or another form of medicine that you take to prevent or to treat an illness; treatment involving drugs
words.example: Are you currently taking any medication?

words.def: in the middle between a larger and smaller size, amount, length, temperature, etc.
words.example: There are three sizes—small, medium and large.

words.def: to become or make something become liquid as a result of heating
words.example: The snow showed no sign of melting.

words.def: the state of being a member of a group, a club, an organization, etc.
words.example: membership of something, (British English), Who is eligible to apply for membership of the association?

words.def: worth remembering or easy to remember, especially because of being special or unusual
words.example: The holiday provided many memorable moments.

words.def: a word or phrase used to describe somebody/something else, in a way that is different from its normal use, in order to show that the two things have the same qualities and to make the description more powerful, for example She has a heart of stone; the use of such words and phrases
words.example: a game of football used as a metaphor for the competitive struggle of life
